mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth above 100k in a month signals robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 100k monthly growth.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in GDP and stable inflation (CPI) indicates a healthy economic expansion with favorable valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 50% of periods where both GDPC1 exceeds 2% YoY and CPIAUCSL remains below 2% YoY.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals increased manufacturing activity that boosts corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 40% of instances where INDPRO shows consecutive monthly increases.

mechanism_narrative: Monetary tightening above 4.5% federal funds rate signals reduced liquidity and higher discount rates, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment below 8.5% indicates a tightening labor market that pressures wage growth and corporate margins, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of instances where UNRATE falls below 8.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate strong demand for residential construction, boosting related industries and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in > 40% of instances where HOUST shows consecutive monthly increases.

mechanism_narrative: Combined growth in retail sales and housing starts signals strong consumer demand and economic optimism, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of periods where both RSAFS exceeds 380k and HOUST exceeds 600k.
